Off Calendar Jul-31-2012 Granted Per Written Order

Off Calendar Jul-02-2012 Off Calendar

Petition To Establish Claim Of Ownership To Personal Property And For Damages Due To Elder Abuse
Off Calendar Jun-26-2012 Continued To Jul-31-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Establish Claim Of Ownership To Personal Property And For Damages Due To Elder Abuse
Off Calendar Mar-27-2012 Continued To Jun-26-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Vaught for Petitioner. J.P. Harbour for Respondent.

Counsel reported that Discovery is pending and that they are not yet ready for mediation. The Petition to Establish Claim of Ownership, etc. is continued to June 26, 2012 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 for status of readiness for setting.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy F. Brown.

Petition To Establish Claim Of Ownership To Personal Property And For Damages Due To Elder Abuse
Off Calendar Feb-14-2012 Continued To Mar-27-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Establish Claim Of Ownership To Personal Property And For Damages Due To Elder Abuse
Off Calendar Dec-19-2011 Continued To Feb-14-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Establish Claim Of Ownership To Personal Property And For Damages Due To Elder Abuse
Off Calendar Oct-17-2011 Continued To Dec-19-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Establish Claim Of Ownership To Personal Property And For Damages Due To Elder Abuse
Off Calendar Jul-11-2011 Continued To Oct-17-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jon Vaught Litigation Counsel for Mary Sloup. Darrell Sooy for Bank of East Asia. Alexis Donoghoe and John Harbour for Robert Tacchetto and Martha Tacchetto.

The Bank will continue to hold the funds as if they were Interpleaders. Mr. Vaught is to prepare the order regarding the funds. Discussions were held regarding how to proceed (discovery/go to trial). The matter was continued to October 17, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of the petition and status of discovery. The Bank does not need to appear on that date and will continue to receive notice of all pleadings/motions. Jennifer Jaynes Counsel for the Estate is to receive notice as well.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.
